summary: The test suite demonstrates good Pester methodology and organization (BeforeAll patterns, proper isolation with GUIDs, tagging, skip logic) but has significant brittleness and incompleteness issues. DependencyLicenseChecker.Tests.ps1 unit tests are well-structured with good error case coverage; however, Workflow.Tests.ps1 integration tests are fragile—they validate output via rigid regex patterns (e.g., matching exact summary strings like 'Total: 4  Approved: 2') which will break on minor output formatting changes. Critical fixture files referenced by tests are missing (fixtures/case1/package.json, fixtures/case2/requirements.txt, fixtures/license-*.json, .github/workflows/dependency-license-checker.yml, src/DependencyLicenseChecker.psm1, .actrc), making the suite untestable as provided. While the test design is sound (good isolation, proper mocking demonstrated, reasonable coverage of requirements), the absence of fixtures and reliance on exact console output matching undermines regression-detection confidence. The act integration layer is overly brittle—it should parse JSON reports rather than scraping formatted text from stdout.
